rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in binary expressions by ensuring left and right sides of '=' have identical types, matching visible AADL data types.
When generating AGREE contracts, use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid scope resolution errors.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always align helper declaration types with the types of their assigned expressions.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes that are not present in the input AADL model.
For implementation-level contracts, use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL features or previously declared helpers within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within component type or implementation annexes.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid using AADL type names like 'integer' or 'boolean'.
anti_patterns: Do not write guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type annexes for such contracts.
Do not introduce new package references or data types in AGREE code that are not declared in the AADL model.
